talk_title: KEAP1-NRF2 mediated resistance to KRAS inhibition and glutamine metabolism targeting

targets.dependency_context: KRAS G12D inhibitor treated PDAC and NSCLC models

MOA_summary: Loss of KEAP1 stabilizes NRF2, reprogramming transcription and glutamine metabolism to confer resistance to KRAS G12D and pan‑RAS inhibitors; co‑inhibition of glutamine metabolism with DRP104 restores sensitivity in vitro and in vivo.
key_findings: CRISPR screens identified KEAP1 knockout as a top driver of resistance to KRAS G12D (MRTX1133) and pan‑RAS (RMC7977) inhibitors.
KEAP1 KO cells show NRF2 and NQO1 up‑regulation and reduced sensitivity to KRAS inhibitors in both pancreatic and NSCLC models.
A glutamine antagonist (DRP104) combined with KRAS inhibitors suppresses tumor growth in three resistant in vivo models.
Patient and PDX datasets reveal higher expression of a KEAP1‑deficiency gene signature in KRAS inhibitor‑resistant cases.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: KEAP1 loss drives resistance via NRF2‑dependent transcriptional program distinct from RAS, MYC, TEAD pathways.

combination_strategies.rationale: Target glutamine metabolism up‑regulated by KEAP1 loss to overcome KRAS inhibitor resistance
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor growth inhibition in PDAC PDX, lung allograft, and KRAS‑mutant models

talk_title: Selective ULK1/2 inhibitor 2088 synergizes with KRAS pathway blockade
MOA_summary: The ULK1/2 inhibitor 2088 blocks autophagy initiation, enhancing the efficacy of KRAS pathway inhibitors (MEK or KRAS G12C inhibitors) by preventing drug‑tolerant persister cell emergence without clonal selection.
key_findings: 2088 shows higher cellular affinity than existing ULK1 inhibitors in NanoBRET assays.
Combination of 2088 with trametinib (MEK inhibitor) or sotorasib (KRAS G12C inhibitor) yields strong synergy in NSCLC H2030 cells.
In vivo, 2088 plus sotorasib significantly improves tumor growth inhibition compared to monotherapy in Calu‑1 xenografts.
DNA barcode tracking indicates the combination delays clonal outgrowth and reduces drug‑tolerant persister populations.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: ULK1 inhibition overcomes adaptive autophagy‑mediated resistance; no specific resistance to 2088 observed.

combination_strategies.rationale: Block autophagy that supports survival of KRAS‑inhibited cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced tumor regression in xenograft models

combination_strategies.rationale: Target parallel MAPK signaling while suppressing autophagy

talk_title: Modeling acquired resistance to pan‑KRAS inhibitor RMC‑6236 in pancreatic cancer
MOA_summary: KRAS Y64 mutations disrupt binding of the pan‑KRAS molecular glue RMC‑6236, conferring resistance; resistance can be overcome by MEK inhibition, suggesting downstream MAPK blockade as a viable combination.
key_findings: Y64D mutation abolishes RMC‑6236 binding to KRAS and prevents tri‑complex formation with RAF.
KRAS Y64D engineered cell lines show >50‑fold increase in IC50 to RMC‑6236 compared with wild‑type.
In xenografts, Y64D tumors exhibit markedly reduced response to RMC‑6236 at both low and high doses.
MEK inhibitor restores sensitivity in Y64D mutant models, indicating downstream MAPK blockade can bypass resistance.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Resistance driven by a single point mutation (Y64) that blocks molecular glue engagement; not due to KRAS amplification.

combination_strategies.rationale: Bypass KRAS‑mediated resistance caused by Y64 mutation
combination_strategies.phenotype: Restored tumor growth inhibition in Y64D models

talk_title: Combining RAS inhibitors with ribonucleotide reductase inhibitor BBI 825 to prevent ecDNA‑driven resistance

biomarkers.name: KRAS amplification (ecDNA)
biomarkers.finding: High baseline ecDNA correlates with rapid resistance emergence

models.name: RW7213
models.genotype_or_key_features: MSS CRC, ecDNA+, KRAS amplification prone

models.name: CLR142
models.genotype_or_key_features: KRAS G12C CRC, acquired KRAS amplification at progression

MOA_summary: Inhibition of ribonucleotide reductase with BBI 825 limits nucleotide pools, suppressing ecDNA‑driven KRAS amplification and delaying or preventing resistance to KRAS inhibitors in colorectal cancer models.
key_findings: Combination of BBI 825 with adagrasib or sotorasib completely prevented resistance emergence in ecDNA‑positive RW7213 cells.
BBI 825 suppressed KRAS amplification in resistant subclones and reduced MEK amplification in deraxurib‑treated cells.
In RW7213 xenografts, BBI 825 plus adagrasib markedly delayed tumor growth and lowered KRAS amplification frequency.
In CLR142 PDX, addition of BBI 825 enhanced efficacy of sotorasib plus cetuximab and reduced c‑MYC expression.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Resistance driven by ecDNA‑mediated oncogene amplification; BBI 825 selectively impairs this process.

combination_strategies.rationale: Prevent KRAS amplification–driven resistance in ecDNA‑positive CRC
combination_strategies.phenotype: No resistant clones detected in vitro

combination_strategies.rationale: Same as above; block nucleotide supply for ecDNA replication
combination_strategies.phenotype: Significant delay of resistance in RW7213 xenografts

talk_title: JAK‑STAT pathway inhibition sensitizes KRAS G12D mutant pancreatic cancer to KRAS inhibitors

speaker.name: Yi Zhao
speaker.affiliation: Ruijin Hospital, Shanghai Jiao Tong University School of Medicine

biomarkers.name: phospho‑STAT3 (Y705)
biomarkers.finding: Higher baseline p‑STAT3 correlates with resistance to KRAS G12D inhibitor

models.name: MRT1133‑resistant PDAC line
models.genotype_or_key_features: KRAS G12D, acquired resistance, high p‑STAT3

clinical_data.population: advanced PDAC patients receiving HRS‑4642 + gemcitabine + pardusol

MOA_summary: KRKRAS G12D inhibition induces acute STAT3 activation via IL‑6 feedback; STAT3 inhibition restores sensitivity and synergizes with KRAS inhibitors in resistant pancreatic cancer models.
key_findings: Acquired KRAS G12D inhibitor‑resistant PDAC cells display up‑regulated JAK1/STAT3 signaling.
An oral STAT3 inhibitor selectively kills KRAS‑resistant cells and synergizes with KRAS G12D inhibitor in vitro.
Combination of KRAS inhibitor and STAT3 inhibitor leads to durable tumor regressions in KPC CDX models, overcoming relapse seen with KRAS inhibitor alone.
JAK1 inhibitor tofacitinib also blocks phospho‑STAT3 and enhances KRAS inhibitor efficacy.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: STAT3 activation acts as both acute and chronic resistance mechanism to KRAS inhibition.

combination_strategies.rationale: Target STAT3 activation that mediates resistance
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor regression in murine PDAC models

combination_strategies.rationale: Block upstream JAK1 to suppress STAT3 phosphorylation
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced cell viability reduction in combination

talk_title: VEGFR2 blockade overcomes KRAS G12D inhibitor resistance driven by PI3Kγ activation
MOA_summary: Acquired resistance to KRAS G12D inhibition activates a KRAS‑PI3Kγ‑AKT‑SP1 axis that drives VEGF‑A secretion and autocrine VEGFR2 signaling; VEGFR2 blockade with DC101 restores sensitivity.
key_findings: Resistant PDOs show increased VEGF‑A expression and phospho‑VEGFR2 activation.
CRISPR‑mediated VEGFR2 knockout or DC101 treatment sensitizes resistant cells to MRTX1133.
PI3Kγ inhibition reduces VEGF‑A/VEGFR2 signaling and re‑sensitizes resistant models.
Combination of MRTX1133 with DC101 markedly suppresses tumor growth in multiple resistant xenograft models.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Resistance mediated by a KRAS‑PI3Kγ‑SP1‑VEGF‑VEGFR2 autocrine loop; targeting VEGFR2 overcomes this specific pathway.

combination_strategies.rationale: Block autocrine VEGF‑VEGFR2 signaling sustaining resistance
combination_strategies.phenotype: Significant tumor growth inhibition in resistant xenografts

combination_strategies.rationale: Disrupt upstream driver of VEGF‑A production
combination_strategies.phenotype: Decreased VEGF‑A secretion and restored KRAS inhibitor sensitivity

talk_title: KRAS G12C inhibition induces chromosomal instability creating a vulnerability to Aurora kinase A inhibition

targets.dependency_context: KRAS G12C inhibitor‑induced CIN high NSCLC lines

biomarkers.name: micronuclei formation
biomarkers.finding: Higher induction correlates with synergy between KRAS G12C and AURKA inhibition

MOA_summary: KRAS G12C inhibition induces DNA damage and micronuclei formation in a subset of NSCLC cells, activating ATR/ATM pathways that stabilize cyclin B1; Aurora kinase A inhibition forces mitotic catastrophe in these cells.
key_findings: KRAS G12C inhibitor LY3499446 induces variable DNA damage and micronuclei across NSCLC lines.
Aurora kinase A inhibitor LSN3321213 shows the strongest synergy with KRAS G12C inhibition, especially in micronuclei‑high cells.
Combination treatment leads to prolonged G2/M arrest and mitotic catastrophe, markedly reducing colony formation.
In vivo, low‑dose AURKA inhibition combined with KRAS G12C inhibitor produces tumor regression and extends relapse‑free survival.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Synthetic vulnerability limited to KRAS‑inhibitor‑induced chromosomal instability; not observed in CIN‑low lines.

combination_strategies.rationale: Exploit mitotic dependency created by KRAS‑inhibitor‑induced CIN
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor regression at nanomolar AURKA inhibitor doses
